scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: Hands the agent a complete map: identifies Rails monorepo, names ActiveRecord::Relation at relation.rb:5 as the lazy query value, lists the relation/ subdir contents, and points to the entry points (Scoping::Named#all named.rb:22, default_scoped :45, Core#relation core.rb:442). Agent can pick an entry with only targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Paths are concrete with file:line throughout: 'activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:5', the relation/ subdir enumerated by file (query_methods.rb, finder_methods.rb, spawn_methods.rb, merger.rb, etc.), and entry-point methods named with line numbers, not vague descriptions.

justification.rationale: Explains why this is the lazy value ('the lazy query value every finder, scope, and association returns') and points to the @values bag contract that spawn/merge depend on (relation.rb:54-66). Could spell out the lazy-until-enumerated rationale more, but the chaining/spawn reason is stated.

uncertainty.rationale: Closing note flags that 'sense_blast static graph explicitly can't see runtime prepend/plugin extensions' and recommends manual re-check, which is honest scoping; but Step 1 itself doesn't flag uncertainty about how the layers connect during orientation.

relationship.rationale: States the connecting relations: 'finder/scope/association returns a Relation', '#all -> default_scoped -> relation', and that the query interface is composed via include of FinderMethods/QueryMethods/SpawnMethods/etc. (relation.rb:68-70), not just a list of directories.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps the full contract with file:line — include list at relation.rb:68-70, every mixin (FinderMethods, Calculations, SpawnMethods, QueryMethods, Batches, Explain, Delegation, SignedId/TokenFor RelationMethods, Enumerable) tied to its file, plus the @values constants at relation.rb:54-66 that spawn/merge depend on. An agent could change the contract knowing what to preserve.

specificity.rationale: Pieces are named concretely with file:line: 'SpawnMethods @ relation/spawn_methods.rb:8', 'QueryMethods @ relation/query_methods.rb:9', 'Delegation @ relation/delegation.rb', plus value-constants at :54-66 and init at :78 — no vague 'the query modules' phrasing.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY succinctly: constants 'define the @values-bag contract every spawn/merge depends on', and the build/execute order shows chaining mutates @values then spawn clones. The composition rationale is implicit rather than spelled out as 'focused modules', but the role of each piece is clear.

uncertainty.rationale: The closing note flags that sense_blast 'explicitly can't see runtime prepend/plugin extensions' so encryption prepend and third-party gems 'should be re-checked manually' — calibrated uncertainty for a contract piece. Doesn't explicitly flag any module method it didn't open.

relationship.rationale: States the binding relation per piece: 'Relation includes (relation.rb:69-70)' the list, SpawnMethods supplies spawn/merge that 'clones the relation' and 'combine conditions', Delegation produces 'generated methods', and the build->execute order is written as a connected chain (chained setters -> spawn clones -> merge -> arel memoizes -> to_sql -> exec). Per-mixin role is named, not just file location.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: step3_build_execute_order lays out the full path with file:line in order — all/scope (named.rb:22) → QueryMethods setters → spawn (spawn_methods.rb:9-11) → merge (Merger) → arel/build_arel (query_methods.rb:1673,1828) → to_sql (relation.rb:1243) → exec_queries/exec_main_query (:1436,:1460) → _query_by_sql (querying.rb:67) → adapter select_all. An agent could follow that path with only small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Pieces are named exactly with file:line: `SpawnMethods#spawn @ spawn_methods.rb:9-11`, `relation_with :72`, `Merger/HashMerger merger.rb:6,17,46`, `build_arel query_methods.rb:1828`, `exec_main_query relation.rb:1460`. No vague "the query code".

justification.rationale: Mentions that spawn clones and arel memoizes ("QueryMethods#arel memoizes build_arel"), implying immutability/lazy execution, but doesn't explicitly call out WHY chaining spawns a fresh relation (immutability) or WHY execution is deferred — those are implied by the ordering rather than stated.

uncertainty.rationale: The closing note flags that `sense_blast` can't see runtime `prepend` and third-party extensions, but doesn't flag uncertainty specifically about the build/execute path's spawn/merge semantics or the lazy memoization boundary.

relationship.rationale: step3 is written as an explicit numbered chain with arrows — "chaining spawns a new relation → merge combines conditions → arel memoizes build_arel → to_sql/exec_queries → _query_by_sql → adapter select_all" — clearly a connected route, not a file list.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Relation contract

map_quality.rationale: Enumerates the full scattered set grouped by area: query-interface modules, the hard-to-grep call sites (connection_handling.rb:419, sanitization.rb:214, integration.rb:164, encryption ExtendedDeterministicQueries:28, signed_id.rb:33, join_dependency.rb:189, scoping/named.rb:192), plus cross-gem dependents (ActiveSupport Enumerable#pluck/pick, railties QueryCommand). Agent could review every affected dependent with only targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent has a concrete file:line and a one-phrase use-description (e.g. "`Relation === value` @ sanitization.rb:214", "prepend RelationQueries @ extended_deterministic_queries.rb:28", "Enumerable#pick @ :161"). Almost no vague "various helpers" prose.

justification.rationale: Each dependent is paired with WHY it's affected (executes under a role, inlines SQL via type test, computes cache key, rewrites where-values, renders relation), grouped by area rather than dumped as a flat list. Could go slightly deeper on a couple of the predicate_builder entries.

uncertainty.rationale: Closing note explicitly flags what static analysis misses: "sense_blast static graph explicitly can't see runtime prepend/plugin extensions, so the encryption prepend and any third-party gems… should be re-checked manually before merge." Good calibration for the agent.

relationship.rationale: Connecting relation is stated per dependent: `is_a? Relation` (connection_handling), `Relation === value` (sanitization), `prepend RelationQueries` (encryption), case-test in relation.rb:1290, AS Enumerable mirror, CLI renderer. The separate `case_or_shortname_type_tests` group makes the unqualified reach paths explicit — exactly the chain a name-search misses.

steps.step: Trace how relation behavior is reused for associations

map_quality.rationale: Step 5 names CollectionProxy < Relation (collection_proxy.rb:31), Association#target_scope (association.rb:314-315), AssociationScope#scope (association_scope.rb:21), CollectionAssociation#scope (collection_association.rb:302), and the disable-joins variant — a complete map an agent can act on with small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Every association piece is cited with file:line: collection_proxy.rb:31, association.rb:314-315, association_scope.rb:21, collection_association.rb:302, and disable_joins_association_relation.rb:4, with no vague references.

justification.rationale: The summary states the collection 'behaves as relation' and that target_scope merges scope_for_association, but only briefly explains WHY (so association queries inherit the chainable interface) rather than developing the rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: Step 5 itself flags no unknowns about the association reuse, though the closing note about prepend/plugin extensions provides some general caveat; nothing specific about the disable-joins or scope_for_association path being unverified.

relationship.rationale: Connections are stated explicitly: CollectionProxy SUBCLASSES Relation, target_scope BUILDS AssociationRelation.create.merge!(scope_for_association), AssociationScope#scope CONSTRUCTS constraints — the chain from association collection → relation → scope build is traced rather than just listed.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer assesses the full blast radius across every area — subclasses, query-build/exec core, type-test guards, prepend-based interception, feature mixins, cross-gem mirrors, and named-scope wiring — with file:line throughout and a ranked risk hierarchy ('HIGHEST: 3 subclasses... HIGH: type-test guards... MEDIUM cross-gem'). The agent could act safely from this map.

specificity.rationale: Every at-risk dependent is named concretely with file:line and a stated reason: 'connection_handling.rb:419, sanitization.rb:214, database_statements.rb:803, scoping/default.rb:132' for silent type guards; 'enumerable.rb:145,161' for the cross-gem mirror; 'extended_deterministic_queries.rb:28' for the prepend.

justification.rationale: Risk levels are tied to WHY: subclasses 'inherit spawn/merge/exec contract', type-test guards are 'silent' via ===/is_a?, encryption is risky because 'prepend... depends on method shape'. Distinguishes high-risk spawn/merge sites from lower-risk renderers.

uncertainty.rationale: Explicit caveat: 'the sense_blast static graph explicitly can't see runtime prepend/plugin extensions, so the encryption prepend and any third-party gems reaching Relation should be re-checked manually before merge' — flags exactly the dependents structural analysis misses.

relationship.rationale: Each at-risk dependent is tied to its connecting relation — 'reached by ===/is_a? (silent)', 'prepend-based interception', 'subclasses inherit spawn/merge/exec contract', 'AS Enumerable mirror parity' — rather than a flat ranked file list with bare risk labels.

map_quality.rationale: Delivers a complete map: 4 core files to edit (relation.rb, spawn_methods.rb, merger.rb, query_methods.rb), dependents grouped by area with file:line via step4 reference, and a concrete test list spanning AR + AS + railties. A teammate could land the change from this alone.

specificity.rationale: Every file is concrete with file:line (e.g., 'spawn_methods.rb (spawn/merge)', 'relations_test.rb', 'enumerable_test.rb (pluck/pick mirror)', 'railties/test query command coverage'); insertion points named by method (build_arel/arel, spawn/merge).

justification.rationale: Each edited file is tagged with its role ('init/values/exec', 'spawn/merge', 'arel/build_arel') and dependents are bucketed by area; could spell out more explicitly WHY each test file exists in the map, but the area grouping carries most of the why.

uncertainty.rationale: Closing note flags that sense_blast 'can't see runtime prepend/plugin extensions' and that encryption prepend + third-party gems should be re-checked — direct uncertainty call-out tied to the riskiest dependents.

relationship.rationale: Map connects each file via its relation: spawn/merge for spawn_methods, arel/build_arel for query_methods, ===/is_a? guards for scattered sites, prepend interception for encryption, AS Enumerable mirror — the chain from the Relation change to each verified file is explicit, though a few test files are listed without their connecting path.
